User Manual

Version 1.0 — March 2026 · For Caretakers, Site Managers & UK Administrators

Overview

The CCD Orphanage Portal replaces the previous Excel workbook system for tracking expenses across City Centre Dawah's orphanages in Uganda, Gambia, and Indonesia.

Caretaker sends message
via WhatsApp or Telegram
➔
System processes &
logs expense
➔
UK Admin reviews
in Django Admin

The system supports:

  • Dual messaging channels: WhatsApp and Telegram
  • Multi-currency: Local amounts (UGX, GMD, IDR) automatically converted to GBP
  • Budget guardrails: Automatic warnings when spending approaches or exceeds limits
  • Receipt photos: Attach photos of receipts alongside expense messages
  • Reports dashboard: Interactive charts and budget gauges
  • PDF reports: Monthly summary and budget vs actual, downloadable
  • Audit trail: Every action is logged for transparency

User Roles

RoleAccessTypical Person
CaretakerSend expenses via WhatsApp/Telegram onlyOrphanage staff on the ground
Site ManagerView expenses for their site via AdminLocal team lead per orphanage
AdminFull system access — all sites, budgets, usersUK-based CCD finance team
ViewerRead-only Admin accessBoard members, auditors

Message Format

Every expense follows the same simple format:

Category Amount Description

Examples:

MessageCategoryAmountDescription
Food 50000 rice KalerweFood50,000rice Kalerwe
Medical 25000 clinic visitMedical25,000clinic visit
Utilities 15000Utilities15,000(none)
Education 100000 school fees term 2Education100,000school fees term 2
Transportation 8000 fuelTransportation8,000fuel
Rules Category must come first (one word, case-insensitive). Amount must be the second word (numbers only, no currency symbol). Description is optional — everything after the amount. Commas in amounts are accepted. Always enter amounts in your local currency (UGX, GMD, or IDR) — the system automatically converts to GBP.

Expense Categories

Use these categories exactly as shown (small typos are tolerated):

Food
Salaries
Utilities
Medical
Clothing
Education
Maintenance
Transportation
Renovations
Contingency
CategoryWhat it covers
FoodAll food purchases, groceries, meals
SalariesStaff wages, helper payments
UtilitiesElectricity, water, gas, internet
MedicalClinic visits, medicine, health supplies
ClothingChildren's clothing, shoes, uniforms
EducationSchool fees, books, stationery
MaintenanceRepairs, cleaning supplies, minor fixes
TransportationFuel, bus fares, vehicle maintenance
RenovationsMajor building work, construction
ContingencyUnplanned or emergency expenses
Typo tolerance The system accepts near-exact typos (e.g. "Foood" → Food, "Educaton" → Education). If your spelling is too far off, you'll be asked to resend with the correct category.

Sending via WhatsApp

1
Open WhatsApp and go to the CCD Expense Bot conversation
2
Type your expense: Food 50000 rice Kalerwe
3
Press Send and wait for the confirmation reply (usually within seconds)
First time? Your phone number must be registered in the system by your admin before you can submit expenses. If you get a "not registered" error, contact your site manager.

Sending via Telegram

1
Open Telegram and search for the CCD Expense Bot
2
Press Start to activate the bot (first time only)
3
Type your expense: Food 50000 rice Kalerwe
4
Press Send and wait for the confirmation reply
First time? Your Telegram username must be registered in the system. Make sure you have a username set (Settings → Username).

Attaching Receipts

WhatsApp

  1. Tap the camera or attachment icon
  2. Take a photo of the receipt or choose from gallery
  3. In the caption field, type your expense: Food 50000 rice Kalerwe
  4. Send

Telegram

  1. Tap the attachment icon (paperclip)
  2. Choose a photo of the receipt
  3. In the caption field, type your expense: Food 50000 rice Kalerwe
  4. Send
Note The receipt photo is stored alongside the expense and visible to UK admins during review.

Understanding Replies

Successful expense

*Expense Logged* #142

*Category:* Food
*Amount:* 50000 UGX (10.00 GBP)
*Receipt:* attached

This tells you:

  • #142 — unique reference number
  • Category: Food — budget category it was filed under
  • Amount: 50000 UGX (10.00 GBP) — local amount and converted GBP value
  • Receipt: attached (or "none" if no photo was sent)

Error — invalid format

Send expenses as:
Category Amount [description]

Example: Food 50000 rice Kalerwe

Error — unrecognised category

Category 'Fuud' not recognised.
Valid categories: Clothing, Contingency, Education, Food, Maintenance,
Medical, Renovations, Salaries, Transportation, Utilities

Error — not registered

Your number (+256XXX...) is not registered.
Contact your site manager to register.

Budget Warnings

When your expense pushes a category's annual budget past a threshold:

At 80% of budget

*Expense Logged* #143

*Category:* Food
*Amount:* 50000 UGX (10.00 GBP)
*Receipt:* none
⚠ Budget alert: Food is at 83% (£340.00 remaining of £2,000.00)

At 100% (budget exceeded)

*Expense Logged* #144

*Category:* Food
*Amount:* 50000 UGX (10.00 GBP)
*Receipt:* none
⚠ BUDGET EXCEEDED: Food is at 105% (£2,100.00 of £2,000.00)
Important Budget warnings do NOT block your expense. The expense is always saved. The warning alerts you and your admin so they can investigate.

Common Mistakes and Fixes

ProblemWhat you sentFix
Category missing50000 riceAdd category first: Food 50000 rice
Amount not a numberFood fifty thousandUse digits: Food 50000
Wrong category spellingFuud 50000Resend as Food 50000
Currency symbol includedFood $50000Remove symbol: Food 50000
Phone not registeredAny messageContact your site manager
No Telegram usernameAny messageSet username in Telegram Settings

Logging In (Site Managers)

1
Go to https://orphanages.ccdawah.org/admin/
2
Log in with your username and password, or click "Sign in with Google" (for @ccdawah.org accounts)

Viewing Expenses

After logging in, click "Expenses" in the left sidebar. Key columns:

  • Expense date — when the expense was incurred
  • Site — which orphanage
  • Category — budget category
  • Amount — GBP with local currency in brackets
  • Budget — colour-coded warning badge
  • Status — Logged / Reviewed / Queried
  • Channel — How the expense was submitted

Use the right-side filter panel to narrow by site, category, status, channel, budget warning, or date.

Budget vs Actual Dashboard

Click "Site budgets" in the sidebar to see every budget line with Annual Amount, Actual Spend, Remaining, and % Used. This replaces the old Excel summary sheet.

Managing Your Team

Ensure all caretakers have their phone numbers (for WhatsApp) and Telegram usernames registered. New caretakers should be onboarded using the Onboarding Guide.

Note You cannot create users yourself — ask a UK admin to create accounts.

Admin Dashboard Overview

SectionModelsPurpose
COREOrganisations, Sites, Users, Budget categories, Funding sources, Project categories, Audit logsMaster data and user management
EXPENSESSite budgets, Expenses, Exchange rates, Projects, Project budgets, Project expensesFinancial tracking
WEBHOOKSWhatsApp messages, Telegram messagesRaw message audit trail

Reviewing Expenses

1
Go to Expenses → Expenses
2
Filter by Status = Logged to see unreviewed expenses
3
Review each expense: check amount, category, receipt, budget warnings
4
Select expenses, choose "Mark as reviewed" from the Action dropdown, click Go

Flagging Expenses for Query

If an expense looks incorrect: select it, choose "Flag for query" from the Action dropdown, and click Go. The caretaker/site manager should then be contacted to clarify.

Managing Budgets

1
Go to Expenses → Site budgets → Add Site budget
2
Select Site, Category, Financial year, and enter Annual amount (GBP)
3
Click Save — budget guardrails take effect immediately

Managing Users

1
Go to Core → Users → Add User
2
Fill in: Username, Password, Organisation, Site, Role, Phone, Telegram username
3
Click Save
Critical for messaging A caretaker cannot submit expenses via WhatsApp unless their phone number is entered. They cannot submit via Telegram unless their telegram_username is entered.

Phone number format: Use E.164 digits without the + prefix:

  • Uganda: 256... (e.g. 256701234567)
  • Gambia: 220... (e.g. 2201234567)
  • Indonesia: 62... (e.g. 628123456789)

Managing Categories and Funding Sources

Budget Categories and Funding Sources are managed under Core in the sidebar.

Warning Changing a category name after expenses have been logged against it will affect historical reporting. Prefer deactivating old categories and creating new ones.

Exchange Rates

The system uses the most recent rate on or before the expense date. Add/update rates under Expenses → Exchange rates.

CurrencyCountryExample Rate
UGXUganda1 GBP = ~5,000 UGX
GMDGambia1 GBP = ~75 GMD
IDRIndonesia1 GBP = ~20,000 IDR
Important If no exchange rate exists for a currency, the expense will be rejected and the caretaker will receive an error message. Always ensure rates are up to date.

Viewing Message Logs

Every incoming message is stored for audit purposes under Messaging in the sidebar. Shows message IDs, sender details, body text, media references, and timestamps.

Audit Trail

Every model save is recorded under Core → Audit logs with timestamp, user, table name, record ID, and action (CREATE/UPDATE). This provides a tamper-evident record of all system activity.

Reports Dashboard

The interactive dashboard at /reports/dashboard/ provides:

SectionWhat it shows
Summary cardsTotal spend (GBP), expense count, flagged expenses
Monthly trendLine chart of month-by-month expenditure
Category breakdownBar chart of spending per category
Channel breakdownDoughnut chart — WhatsApp vs Telegram vs web
Budget gaugesProgress bars per category with status badges
Recent expenses10 most recent with amounts and categories

PDF Reports

Monthly Expense Summary

Go to /reports/monthly-summary/, select Site, Year, Month, then Preview or Download PDF.

Budget vs Actual Report

Go to /reports/budget-vs-actual/, select Site and Year, then Preview or Download PDF.

Managing Projects

Projects track one-off or recurring initiatives (e.g. "Ramadan Food Packs 2026"). Create under Expenses → Projects with site, category, name, budget, timeline, and status (Planned → Active → Completed/Cancelled).

Link expenses to projects via Expenses → Project expenses using the Project dropdown.

REST API (Phase 2)

A REST API exists for the planned Flutter mobile app, using token authentication:

EndpointMethodPurpose
/api/v1/auth/token/POSTGet auth token
/api/v1/sites/GETList sites
/api/v1/expenses/GET/POSTList or create expenses
/api/v1/categories/GETBudget categories
/api/v1/projects/GETList projects
/api/v1/sync/POSTOffline-first sync

Glossary

TermMeaning
GBPBritish Pounds Sterling — the reporting currency
UGXUgandan Shilling
GMDGambian Dalasi
IDRIndonesian Rupiah
ChannelHow the expense was submitted (WhatsApp, Telegram, Web, App, Paper)
LoggedExpense recorded but not yet reviewed
ReviewedExpense checked and approved by admin
QueriedExpense flagged for investigation
Budget guardrailAutomatic warning at 80% or 100% of annual budget
Fuzzy matchingSystem's ability to match near-correct category names
IdempotencySystem prevents duplicate expenses from the same message
ProjectA trackable initiative with its own budget and timeline
{% endblock %}
